# Likelihood Ratio Worked Example

You observe repeated co-posting, similar captions, and no disclosure.

Assign qualitative likelihoods:

| Evidence | P(evidence if coordinated) | P(evidence if independent) | LR |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| repeated tight timing | 0.70 | 0.05 | 14 |
| similar captions | 0.60 | 0.20 | 3 |
| same media hashes | 0.80 | 0.15 | 5.33 |
| no disclosure | 0.50 | 0.70 | 0.71 |

Combined rough LR:

`14 × 3 × 5.33 × 0.71 ≈ 159`

This does not mean "159x guilty." It means the evidence pattern is much more expected under coordination than independence, under these assumed probabilities.

## Caution

Subjective probabilities must be disclosed. Use this as reasoning discipline, not mathematical certainty.
